title: Donor1_Untreated_Uninfected
treatment_protocol_ch1: Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Rv (MTB) was grown from a frozen stock to mid-log phase in 7H9 medium (Becton Dickinson), supplemented with albumin-dextrose-catalase (ADC, Becton Dickinson). Before infection, bacteria were washed three times and re-suspended in 1 ml PBS. Clumps were dissociated by 30 passages through a needle and then allowed to sediment for 5 min. Bacteria in the supernatant were  killed by heating at 90°C during 30min (defined heareafter hk-MTB). The density of bacteria in the supernatant was verified by measuring the OD600. Macrophages were infected with live MTB at a multiplicity of infection 1:1 (one bacterium-per cell), whereas cells were simulated with hk-MTB at a MOI of 5:1. After 18h of stimulation, cells were washed and total RNA was collected. The transcriptome was analyzed by mRNA sequencing.
growth_protocol_ch1: Blood mononuclear cells were isolated by Ficoll-Paque centrifugation (GE Healthcare Life Sciences) from 3 healthy donors. CD14+ monocytes were isolated by positive selection using CD14 microbeads (Miltenyi Biotec) and allowed to differentiate into Mφs in the presence of granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) (20 ng/mL; R&D Systems) over a six-day period. RPMI medium + GM-CSF was added to the cell cultures every two days.
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted using QIAzol reagent (Life Technologies) and purified over RNeasy columns (Qiagen). The quality of all samples was assessed with an Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies) to verify RNA integrity. Only samples with a good RNA yield and no RNA degradation (ratio of 28S to 18S, > 1.7; RNA integrity number > 9) were used for further experiments.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: cDNA libraries were prepared with the Illumina TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit v2 and were sequenced on an llumina HiSeq 4000
data_processing: STAR v2.5.0b was used to map RNA-seq reads to the hg38 reference genome and quantify  gene expression (option--quantMode GeneCounts) by counting the fragments overlapping the Ensembl genes (GRCh38 v. 83).
data_processing.2: Supplementary_files_format_and_content: The raw counts were generated using STAR v2.5.0b for GRCh38 Ensembl genes
